senses.definition: the act of pressing or crushing something to make it flat or soft; the sound or effect of smooshing
examples: The smooshing of the jelly on the toast was satisfying.
The cartoon showed a dramatic smooshing motion as the character squished the clay.

senses.definition: a historically used unit of length used in measuring track length, equal to 5.28 meters in old English units; also a surname or place-name (rare in common usage).
examples: The old road was measured in smoots for the university prank.
Smoot is also the last name of several notable individuals.

senses.definition: past tense of smoot, meaning to measure or arrange using smoot-length units, or to smooth in a rough, humorous, or arbitrary way (nonstandard).
examples: They smooted the path to test the old measurement.
In the prank, they smooted the banner across the wall.

senses.definition: having a flat, even surface with no roughness or bumps
examples: The smooth surface of the table was perfect for writing.
Her skin felt smooth and cool.

senses.definition: to make something flat, even, or free from roughness
examples: Please smooth the plaster before it dries.
She smoothed the wrinkles from the sheet.

senses.definition: capable of being made smooth or smoother
examples: The rough stone is smoothable with enough sanding.
This material is smoothable to a glossy finish.

senses.definition: a species or variety with a smooth back; in some contexts refers to a smooth-backed animal or object, or a name used in craft or fashion for a sleek back design
examples: The smoothback leather has a sleek, uniform finish.
In the aquarium, they labeled a fish as smoothback.

senses.definition: a type of boot with a smooth, slick surface or finish; may refer to a fashion/style descriptor rather than a specific product
examples: He wore smoothboots for the parade.
The label markets smoothboots as tenacious streetwear.

senses.definition: a gun or firearm with a smooth inner bore rather than rifled grooves
examples: The smoothbore musket was common in earlier centuries.
Smoothbore barrels are slower to fire accurately at long range.

senses.definition: having a smooth inner bore or a dull or unremarkable quality; could describe a firearm with a smooth bore or figuratively a dull, unexciting nature
examples: The smoothbored weapon lacks rifling for accuracy.
The metaphor felt smoothbored, not exciting.

senses.definition: plural of smoothbore; firearms with smooth inner barrels
examples: The museum displayed several smoothbores from the era.
Smoothbores were effective at close range.

senses.definition: a coat with a smooth, glossy finish; can refer to fur, hair, or leather with a sleek surface
examples: The dog had a shiny smoothcoat after grooming.
They applied polish to achieve a smoothcoat on the leather.

senses.definition: alternative spelling of smooth (rarely used; primarily historical or dialectal)
examples: They smoothe the course with careful planning.

senses.definition: past tense of smooth; made something flat or even
examples: She smoothed the wrinkles from the sheet.
The ground was smoothed before laying the tiles.

senses.definition: to make something smooth or more even
examples: You can smoothen the cream until it is lump-free.
The road was smoothed by maintenance crews.

senses.definition: past participle of smoothen; made something smoother
examples: The surface was smoothened to prepare for painting.

senses.definition: the act or process of smoothing something; the result of smoothing
examples: Smoothening of the surface took several hours.
The smoothening process improved the finish.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of smoothen; makes smoother
examples: It smoothens the rough edges of the design.
The new coat smoothens out tiny imperfections.

senses.definition: a person or thing that smooths; a tool or device used to make something smooth
examples: The car is a smoother ride after repair.
Use a planer as a smoother to even the wood.

senses.definition: plural of smoother; people or tools that smooth surfaces
examples: Smoothers helped achieve a flawless finish.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of smooth; makes something smooth
examples: She smoothes the fabric before sewing.
The driver smoothes the ride with better suspension.

senses.definition: most smooth; having the least roughness or interruption
examples: This is the smoothest road on the route.
Her skin is the smoothest among the group.

senses.definition: a type of shark or a fish popularly named for a smooth appearance or texture of skin; regional or obsolete usage may vary
examples: The smoothhound is often found near rocky shores.
In the market, buyers discussed catching smoothhounds.

senses.definition: a thick, smooth drink made by blending fruit with milk or yogurt
examples: I had a strawberry smoothie for breakfast.
They blended banana and yogurt into a smoothie.

senses.definition: plural of smoothie; thick fruit drinks
examples: They order smoothies every morning.
There are many flavors of smoothies on the menu.

senses.definition: the process of making something smoother or more uniform; smoothing
examples: The smoothification of the interface improved usability.
Projects focused on the smoothification of data workflows.
